mechanism_narrative: A persistent increase in nonfarm payrolls above trend signals expanding economic activity, boosting corporate earnings expectations and driving S&P 500 returns higher over the following month.
failure_condition: If months with PAYEMS growth > 100k produce 20-day SPX returns that are not significantly positive (mean < 0, t-test p>0.05) over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 3%, the cost of capital increases and discount rates rise, compressing equity valuations and leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If periods with FEDFUNDS above 3% show mean 20-day SPX return >= 0 or rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous industrial production growth above 0.5% month-over-month and a rising fed funds rate indicate an overheating economy that forces tighter monetary policy, dragging equity prices lower over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO > 0.5% monthly and FEDFUNDS rises > 25bp over 3 months
failure_condition: If in months meeting both conditions the mean 60-day SPX return is positive or the interaction term in a linear model is insignificant (p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During the housing boom of the early 2000s, elevated housing starts signal strong residential investment and consumer confidence, pushing equity markets higher over a 20-day horizon as homebuilding-related sectors rally.
failure_condition: If in months when HOUST > 1500 the mean 20-day SPX return is not positive (t-test p>0.05) or if the return distribution does not differ from non-boom periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly drop in retail sales more than 1% signals an immediate demand contraction that reduces earnings forecasts and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: If in months with RSAFS decline >1% the mean 20-day SPX return is positive or rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the consumer price index erodes real purchasing power and forces the Federal Reserve to eventually tighten policy, which depresses equity valuations and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If in periods with positive CPIAUCSL momentum (3-month change > 0.5%) the mean 60-day SPX return is not negative, or the rank IC over 5 years < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent falling unemployment and rising housing starts confirm a strong labor market and robust residential demand, boosting consumer spending and corporate profits, lifting S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls by >0.2pp over 3 months and HOUST rises by >2% over 3 months
failure_condition: If in months meeting the joint trigger the mean 20-day SPX return is not positive (t-test p>0.05) or the interaction coefficient in a regression is insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth exceeds 3% annualized, aggregate demand expands faster than potential output, boosting corporate earnings and driving S&P 500 returns higher over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If in quarters with GDP growth >3% the mean 60-day SPX return is not positive (t-test p>0.10) or the hit rate is below 55% over a 10-year rolling window.
